arguments.argument: Justice demands that we uphold the rights of creators and innovators, even when the potential benefits of violating those rights might seem compelling.

arguments.argument: Intellectual property rights exist to incentivize innovation by ensuring that creators retain control over their work and can derive value from it.

arguments.argument: Justice requires consistency—applying the same rules to all, even when exceptions seem tempting.
